title: TitanSeal Sealants Launch — Logo-System Extension, Swappable SKU Label/Carton Data-Merge, and Print-Ready + Digital Asset Package
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the real Upwork brief: Dubai B2B trading business launching first private-label sealants brand "TitanSeal" (Jan 2027); master logo already designed/approved; need it extended into color/mono/reversed variants + a horizontal lockup; brand-standards PDF with Pantone/CMYK/RGB/Hex; wrap-around cartridge labels for two 600ml grey SKUs (MS Polymer, PU Construction) built as a swappable-element template so SKU codes change easily; a 300ml white Neutral Silicone tube label in the same system; single-color black-on-cardboard outer cartons for 20-cartridge and 24-tube packs; and a digital pack (Amazon UAE, 1080x1080 social tiles, 1920x1080 web banner, 350x120 email signature, WhatsApp catalog cards). REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-connector benchmark: (a) the client is treated as supplying not just the logo PNG but the two desktop-authored print mechanicals the connector legitimately needs — an InDesign cartridge/tube label .indd carrying real <<SKU_NAME>>/<<SKU_CODE>>/<<VOLUME>>/<<COLORWAY>> merge fields, and an Illustrator carton .ai with named Variables — plus a sku_variants.csv; this is the only honest way the connector can produce the "swappable-element, SKU codes easily changed" template the brief explicitly demands (InDesign data-merge / Illustrator Variables). (b) The logo-system VARIANTS (mono, reversed/knockout, color-overlay, clean SVG, horizontal-lockup canvas) are derived from the supplied logo via real raster/vector ops rather than redrawn from scratch. (c) The brand-standards PDF body and final layout authoring (typography pages, usage rules, Amazon listing geometry) and any from-scratch page composition are explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for the connector and flagged as human/desktop finishing — the connector delivers the asset system, the merged SKU mechanicals, the export-ready PDFs/PNGs, and the correctly-sized digital tiles.
one_line_ask: Take the approved TitanSeal master logo plus the client's authored InDesign label .indd and Illustrator carton .ai mechanicals, derive the full logo-variant system, data-merge the three SKUs into swappable print-ready label/carton artwork, and output the print PDFs plus a correctly-sized digital-asset pack.
full_brief:
TitanSeal is a Dubai-based B2B private-label sealants brand launching three SKUs in January 2027. The master logo is approved and supplied as a high-res transparent PNG; the client also supplies two desktop-authored print mechanicals carrying real merge fields/variables (an InDesign wrap label .indd and an Illustrator carton .ai) and a sku_variants.csv with the three SKUs (600ml MS Polymer grey, 600ml PU Construction grey, 300ml Neutral Silicone white). 

Using ONLY the Adobe connector, the freelancer must: (1) upload the supplied logo and mechanicals into a project workspace; (2) build the LOGO-VARIANT SYSTEM from the one master logo — a clean vector SVG (for scalable lockups), a single-color BLACK mono version and a single-color WHITE reversed/knockout version (for the black-on-cardboard carton and dark backgrounds), and a brand-blue color-overlay version — without redrawing the mark; (3) build the wide HORIZONTAL LOCKUP working canvas by cropping/reframing the square logo composition to a 3:1 banner band (a gen-free crop of the source, no invented pixels); (4) run the swappable-SKU LABEL data-merge: the authored .indd + sku_variants.csv produce one print-ready label mechanical per SKU so SKU codes/volumes swap by editing the CSV, then export those merged labels to press-ready PDF; (5) run the CARTON data-merge from the authored .ai Variables + CSV for the 20-cartridge and 24-tube single-color cartons and export to PDF/SVG; (6) assemble the DIGITAL pack — license a construction/jobsite background from Adobe Stock, color-grade it on-brand, then crop-and-resize it to the exact platform specs (1080x1080 social tile, 1920x1080 web banner, 350x120 email signature, and a 1:1 WhatsApp catalog card). 

OUT OF SCOPE for the connector (flag as human/desktop finishing): authoring the 5–8pp brand-standards PDF body copy and typography pages, drawing any new layout from scratch, and the Amazon UAE listing page composition. The connector's job is the asset/variant system, the merged swappable SKU mechanicals, the export-ready print PDFs, and the correctly-sized digital tiles.

inputs.name: titanseal_titanseal_master_logo.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A professional industrial-sealants master logo: the single word 'TitanSeal' as the LARGE DOMINANT wordmark in a bold condensed geometric sans-serif, deep industrial blue (#0B3D91), with a small caulk-bead / droplet icon to its left and a small tagline 'INDUSTRIAL SEALANTS' set beneath the wordmark. Flat vector style, crisp clean edges, solid white background, centred, horizontal.
inputs.realism_notes: Must read as a real approved brand logo: solid flat fills only (so vectorize and single-color fills behave), genuine transparent alpha channel, no gradients or shadows that would muddy a mono/reversed knockout, generous transparent padding so the mono/reversed variants and the 3:1 crop reframe have room.

inputs.name: titanseal_label_mechanical.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: Describe/generate an InDesign label mechanical package metadata: a single wrap-around cartridge label artboard (approx 200mm x 95mm + 3mm bleed and 297mm tube variant) authored with REAL InDesign data-merge placeholder fields <<SKU_NAME>>, <<SKU_CODE>>, <<VOLUME>>, <<COLORWAY>>, <<BARCODE>>, plus a linked logo frame and hazard-pictogram zone. Field names exactly match the CSV header.
inputs.realism_notes: This is an AUTHORED [T] template the CLIENT supplies — the connector cannot create merge fields, so it must be listed as a real client-provided .indd whose <<fields>> bind to the CSV. Represent as a genuine InDesign data-merge document, not a flattened PDF.

inputs.name: titanseal_carton_mechanical.ai
inputs.gen_prompt: Describe/generate an Illustrator carton mechanical: a single-color (black) die-line for a corrugated shipping carton with two size variants (20-cartridge and 24-tube), authored with Illustrator named Variables (object/text/visibility) bound to columns SKU_NAME, SKU_CODE, PACK_COUNT, CARTON_SIZE, BATCH so the artwork repopulates per row. Includes die-line, fragile/this-way-up pictograms, and a logo placement using the mono mark.
inputs.realism_notes: AUTHORED [T] Illustrator Variables template supplied by the CLIENT. Single-color black-on-kraft to match the brief's cost constraint. Variables must be pre-defined in the .ai so document_merge_data_vector can bind the CSV.

inputs.name: sku_variants.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV with header SKU_NAME,SKU_CODE,VOLUME,COLORWAY,BARCODE,PACK_COUNT,CARTON_SIZE,BATCH and exactly 3 data rows: 'MS Polymer Sealant',OBC-MS600-GRY,600ml,Grey,6291100000017,20,20-cartridge,B2701; 'PU Construction Sealant',OBC-PU600-GRY,600ml,Grey,6291100000024,20,20-cartridge,B2701; 'Neutral Silicone Sealant',OBC-NS300-WHT,300ml,White,6291100000031,24,24-tube,B2701. Header names must match the .indd <<fields>> and .ai Variables exactly.
inputs.realism_notes: Real UAE-plausible EAN-13 barcodes and the three exact SKUs from the brief. Column names are the binding contract with both authored templates — they must match exactly or the merge fails.

inputs.name: jobsite_background.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal documentary shot of a clean modern construction jobsite detail: a gloved hand running a bead of grey sealant along an aluminium window frame, shallow depth of field, soft natural daylight, real skin pores on the wrist, visible fabric weave on the work glove, subtle dust on the frame, candid not staged, 5000x3500px, no text, lots of clean negative space on the right for later cropping and overlay.
inputs.realism_notes: This is a STAND-IN for an Adobe Stock license in the workflow; generated only so the benchmark is runnable. Needs documentary realism (pores, glove weave, real grain) and deliberate negative space so crop_and_resize to 4 different specs all yield usable framings.

outputs.name: titanseal_logo.svg
outputs.spec: Clean scalable SVG vectorized from the master logo PNG; solid-fill paths, usable in print lockups at any size.

outputs.name: titanseal_logo_mono_black.png
outputs.spec: Single-color solid black version of the mark on transparent background, for the black-on-cardboard carton.

outputs.name: titanseal_logo_reversed_white.png
outputs.spec: Single-color solid white reversed/knockout version on transparent background, for dark/photo backgrounds.

outputs.name: titanseal_logo_brandblue.png
outputs.spec: Brand-blue color-overlay version of the mark on transparent background.

outputs.name: titanseal_horizontal_lockup.png
outputs.spec: 3:1 horizontal-lockup band cropped/reframed from the square logo composition (a horizontal band of the source, no invented pixels, output no larger than the source), centered on the mark; the top/bottom of the square padding is cropped to hit the ratio. For web banner/email use.

outputs.name: titanseal_labels_merged.pdf
outputs.spec: Press-ready multi-page PDF, one page per SKU, from the authored .indd + CSV data-merge; 3mm bleed, swappable SKU codes/volumes via CSV.

outputs.name: titanseal_cartons_merged.pdf
outputs.spec: Single-color black carton artwork PDF for 20-cartridge and 24-tube packs, from the authored .ai Variables + CSV; die-line preserved.

outputs.name: titanseal_social_tile_1080.png
outputs.spec: 1080x1080 on-brand graded social media tile (jobsite background graded + cropped).

outputs.name: titanseal_web_banner_1920x1080.png
outputs.spec: 1920x1080 web banner crop of the graded jobsite asset.

outputs.name: titanseal_email_signature_350x120.png
outputs.spec: 350x120 email-signature crop of the graded jobsite asset.

outputs.name: titanseal_whatsapp_card_1x1.png
outputs.spec: 1:1 WhatsApp catalog product card crop of the graded jobsite asset.

connector_workflow.note: Initialize upload of the client's approved master logo PNG into the project workspace.
Finalize the logo upload to get a stable asset/presigned URL for the chain.
Vectorize the raster logo into a clean scalable SVG for print lockups — no redrawing.
Derive the single-color BLACK mono version for the black-on-cardboard carton.
Overlay solid white to produce the reversed/knockout version for dark/photo backgrounds.
Overlay brand blue (#0B3D91) to produce the on-brand color variant.

Reframe the square logo composition to a 3:1 horizontal-lockup band by cropping the existing 3000x3000 source (a gen-free crop, output never larger than the source, no invented pixels). The crop is centered on the mark to keep the icon+wordmark in the band; accept that the top and bottom of the square padding are cropped away to hit the 3:1 ratio.
Upload the client-authored InDesign label mechanical carrying real <<merge fields>>.
Upload the sku_variants.csv whose headers bind to both authored templates.
InDesign data-merge: authored .indd <<fields>> + CSV → one swappable label mechanical per SKU (codes change by editing the CSV).
Export the merged label .indd to a press-ready multi-page PDF with bleed.
Upload the client-authored Illustrator carton mechanical with named Variables.
Illustrator Variables data-merge: authored .ai Variables + CSV → single-color carton artwork for 20-cartridge and 24-tube packs.
Export the merged carton .ai to press-ready PDF (die-line preserved, single-color black).
Search + license a construction/jobsite stock photo and pull the full-res presigned URL (license before editing).
Auto-tone the licensed jobsite photo as the grading base.
Shift temperature on-brand (cool industrial) to finish the color grade for all digital tiles.
Crop+resize the graded asset to the 1080x1080 social tile spec.
Crop+resize the graded asset to the 1920x1080 web banner spec.
Crop+resize the graded asset to the 350x120 email-signature spec.
Crop+resize the graded asset to a 1:1 WhatsApp catalog card.

chaining_note: Strict output→input chain. The client's master logo is uploaded (1→2) into logo_asset, which fans out into the full variant system: vectorize→SVG (3), monochromatic_tint→black mono (4), color_overlay→white reversed (5), color_overlay→brand-blue (6), and crop_and_resize→3:1 horizontal-lockup band cropped from the square source (7). In parallel the authored .indd (8) and CSV (9) feed document_merge_data_layout (10) whose merged .indd is consumed by document_render_layout to export the press-ready label PDF (11). The authored .ai (12) plus the same CSV (9) feed document_merge_data_vector (13), whose merged .ai is consumed by document_render_vector for the carton PDF (14). The digital pack is its own chain: license_and_download_stock (15) → apply_auto_tone (16) → adjust_color_temperature (17) → the single graded asset jobsite_graded is reused by four crop_and_resize calls (18–21) for the four exact platform specs — every step after the initial uploads consumes a prior step's output, and the deliverable package emerges entirely from the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 21 connector calls, 13 distinct Adobe tools, spanning 3 connector groups (asset/storage, image raster/vector, document layout/vector data-merge+render). It demands the agent recognize the honest connector boundary — that label/carton "swappable-element templates" can only be produced via authored-template [T] data-merge (InDesign data-merge + Illustrator Variables) bound to a header-matched CSV, that logo variants must be DERIVED from the one supplied mark (vectorize / monochromatic_tint / color_overlay / crop_and_resize reframe) rather than redrawn, and that brand-standards-PDF body authoring and Amazon listing composition are out of connector scope and must be flagged. Multiple independent sub-chains (logo system, label merge, carton merge, digital pack) each run to completion, with one graded asset reused across four exact platform crop specs — exercising fan-out, [T] template binding, and [C] export in one runnable benchmark.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned/generative capability remains anywhere in the spec or its prose. Connector_workflow step 7 is image_crop_and_resize (a [C] gen-free reframe that crops the 3000x3000 logo to a 3:1 band, output never larger than the source, no invented pixels), centered on the mark so the crop clips only the square's top/bottom padding to hit the ratio. This is consistent with the titanseal_horizontal_lockup.png output spec, the chaining_note, the difficulty_rationale, and the logo input realism_notes. Remaining ops are all real, non-generative connector actions: vectorize, monochromatic_tint, color_overlay, [T] document_merge_data_layout/vector on client-authored .indd/.ai, document_render_layout/vector (export-only), asset_license_and_download_stock (licensed stock, not text-to-image), auto_tone/color_temperature, crop_and_resize. No canvas extension/pad, upscale, text-to-image, from-scratch art, object-removal-with-reconstruction, or gen-fill.
reverify.notes: All findings resolved: (1) connector_workflow step 7 now uses image_crop_and_resize (an honest gen-free 3:1 lockup crop), matching the reverify note, the titanseal_horizontal_lockup.png output spec, the chaining_note, and the difficulty_rationale — the previously-documented fix is now actually applied to the workflow array; (2) the output spec and step-7 note are reconciled to the crop semantics (a centered horizontal band of the source, top/bottom square padding cropped away to reach 3:1), so executing step 7 produces exactly the promised output and the design concern about clipping the mark is stated explicitly; (3) counts corrected — tool_call_count is 21; the workflow's distinct tools are exactly the 13 in tools_used (image_crop_and_resize already listed), so distinct_adobe_tools is 13, matched in difficulty_rationale. Brand TitanSeal not flagged by QA, left unchanged.
